nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (Daily 08:00 UTC)
nodes.notes: Daily rotation: 33 cards/day from master_card_catalog. With 1,354 variant rows, one full sweep takes ~41 days. Adjust cron if you want a different cadence.

parameters.query:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT m.id AS card_id, m.card_code, m.card_name, m.set_name,
         m.card_number, m.edition,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY m.id) - 1 AS rn,
         COUNT(*) OVER () AS total
    FROM master_card_catalog m
),
params AS (
  SELECT ((CURRENT_DATE - DATE '2026-01-01')::int * 33)
         % (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM master_card_catalog) AS day_offset
)
SELECT r.card_id, r.card_code, r.card_name, r.set_name,
       r.card_number, r.edition
  FROM ranked r
 CROSS JOIN params p
 WHERE (r.rn - p.day_offset + r.total) % r.total < 33
 ORDER BY r.card_id;

nodes.name: Get Daily 33 Cards
nodes.notes: Deterministic rotation by days-since-epoch (2026-01-01). Same set of 33 cards every time the workflow runs on a given date. Wraps the catalog cleanly when stride * days exceeds catalog size.

nodes.name: eBay Finding API (findCompletedItems)
nodes.notes: GET against eBay Finding API. Iterates over each input item — 33 calls per run, sequential with 2s spacing (batchSize=1, batchInterval=2000ms) to stay under the Finding API rate limit. Standard tier limit is 5,000 calls/day; 2s/call → ~66s total wall time per run, ~30 req/min sustained — well under the per-second ceiling. categoryId 2536 = Trading Card Singles.

nodes.name: Parse Sold Comps
nodes.notes: Each input is one card's worth of eBay results. Output one row per individual sold listing. Identity (card_id, card_code, set_name, etc.) inherited from the searched card. grader+grade parsed from title; both NULL if either can't be derived (FK-safe).

parameters.query:
WITH grade_check AS (
  SELECT
    $8::text AS in_grader,
    $9::text AS in_grade,
    CASE
      WHEN $8 IS NULL OR $9 IS NULL THEN TRUE
      WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM master_grade_catalog
                    WHERE grader = $8 AND raw_label = $9) THEN TRUE
      ELSE FALSE
    END AS pair_valid
)
INSERT INTO ebay_transactions (
  item_id, card_id, card_code, card_name, set_name, card_number, edition,
  grader, grade, platform, currency,
  sold_price, listed_price, sold_at, sale_type,
  seller, seller_feedback_pct,
  title_raw, raw_payload, inserted_at
)
SELECT
  $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, COALESCE($7, 'N/A'),
  CASE WHEN gc.pair_valid THEN gc.in_grader ELSE NULL END,
  CASE WHEN gc.pair_valid THEN gc.in_grade  ELSE NULL END,
  'ebay', $10,
  $11, $12, $13::timestamptz, $14,
  $15, $16,
  $17, $18::jsonb, NOW()
FROM grade_check gc
ON CONFLICT (item_id) DO NOTHING;

nodes.name: Insert into ebay_transactions
nodes.notes: ON CONFLICT (item_id) DO NOTHING — eBay item_ids never recycle, so re-runs are safely idempotent. INSERT references only columns confirmed present on live ebay_transactions; listing_url / image_url / condition / seller_feedback / created_at not in this table (they live on ebay_asks). Column `seller` (legacy) carries the parsed seller_username (data-standard §11.4 naming-drift list — convergence pending). grade_check CTE consults master_grade_catalog FK before insert — non-canonical (grader, grade) pairs land as (NULL, NULL) instead of FK-rejecting the whole row, matching the data-standard §1 'rows that fail resolution still insert' pattern.
